message:
feat(core,types,btree,vdbe,vfs): schema-only connections, record profile callsite attribution, read-only flag wiring

Schema-only connections:
- Add Connection::open_schema_only() that loads only database schema
  (tables, indexes, triggers, views) without reading row data into the
  in-memory MemDatabase — makes opening even multi-gigabyte databases
  near-instantaneous when queries use pager-backed B-tree cursors
- Wire SQLITE_OPEN_READ_ONLY flag through open_with_flags() to use
  schema-only mode, making the compat layer flag functional
- Add finalize_read_only_concurrent_commit() for clean read-only
  transaction cleanup without SSI validation overhead

Record profile callsite attribution:
- Add RecordProfileScope enum (Unattributed, CoreConnection,
  CoreCompatPersist, VdbeEngine, VdbeVectorizedScan, BtreeCursor) with
  thread-local scope tracking via Cell and RAII scope guards
- Add per-scope atomic counters for parse_record, parse_record_into,
  and parse_record_column calls with zero overhead when profiling is
  disabled
- Instrument all record-parsing callsites: core Connection::execute,
  compat_persist load, VDBE engine execute, vectorized scan next_batch,
  and BtreeCursor binary search / comparison / rowid operations
- Add RecordProfileScopeBreakdownSnapshot for callsite-level reporting

VFS feature gate:
- Include linux-asupersync-uring in the native feature set so file-backed
  databases use the io_uring path by default

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -9,7 +9,7 @@ rust-version.workspace = true
 
 [features]
 default = ["linux-asupersync-uring"]
-native = ["fsqlite-types/native"]
+native = ["fsqlite-types/native", "linux-asupersync-uring"]
 wasm = ["fsqlite-types/wasm"]
 linux-asupersync-uring = ["native", "dep:asupersync", "dep:pollster"]
 # Deprecated compatibility toggle; guarded by compile_error in src/uring.rs.
